tagline: From a bachelor's degree to Staff Engineer — two promotions, ten years.

start.title: Software Engineer
start.note: You are here — a bachelor's degree, ~0 years of experience. You finish well-defined tasks handed to you.

rungs.title: Senior Software Engineer
rungs.scope: Own a feature or service end-to-end — the jump from finishing tasks to owning a system.
rungs.experience: ~5 years (Google L5 / Meta E5 ≈ 5–9 yrs). The industry's terminal level.
rungs.evaluated: A promo packet showing you already do Senior-scope work: quantified impact, feedback from Senior+ peers, and a manager who will sponsor the case.

rungs.title: Staff / Lead Software Engineer
rungs.scope: Set technical direction several teams build against — the jump from one system to the whole org.
rungs.experience: ~9–12+ years (Google L6 / Meta E6). A promotion gated on multi-team impact, not tenure.
rungs.evaluated: A packet showing sustained multi-team impact (not one project): design docs, RFCs, postmortems, and heavily-weighted Staff+ sponsor endorsements, calibrated above your team.
